transcript.text: I'm an automated medical assistant. If this is an emergency, hang up and call 911. Do you consent to continue? (yes/no)
Yes.
What is your main medical concern today?
I have low back pain.
The next few questions may feel repetitive, but they are important for medication safety and treatment decisions. How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Hello? Hello?
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
All right.
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
It is
How old are you? Please say your age in years.
Do you have any medication allergies? If yes, list them. If no, say no.
Forty-seven
What sex were you assigned at birth? Please say male or female.
No.
Is pregnancy possible? (yes/no/unknown)
Male
Have you ever been told you have kidney disease or significantly reduced kidney function? (yes/no/not sure)
No, since I said no.
Have you ever been told you have liver disease (such as hepatitis, cirrhosis, fatty liver, or abnormal liver tests)? (yes/no/not sure)
No.

It sounds like you’re experiencing nonspecific low back pain, which is quite common and often related to muscle strain or other non-serious causes. Because this is a phone visit, I can’t examine you or run tests, but based on what you told me, medication treatment like naproxen or acetaminophen may be appropriate. A licensed physician will review this note and, if suitable, may prescribe the recommended medication. If your pain worsens, or if you develop new symptoms like weakness, numbness, trouble controlling your bladder or bowels, or fever, please seek in-person care the same day. For severe neurological symptoms, go to the emergency room right away. If you need an in-person exam or testing, please visit urgent care or your primary care clinician. A physician will review your case and guide next steps.
